sglang.title: fix bailing_moe with enable_dp_attention
sglang.author: GuoweiWangU
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Inference hang with dp attention.
python -m sglang.launch_server --model {path_to_bailing_moe} --tp 4 --dp 2 --enable-dp-attention --enable-dp-lm-head
The core reason is the #5558 , which leads to embedding forward errors.
Modifications
Use enable_tp instead of use_attn_tp_group for Vocab

sglang.title: router-grpc: Support jinja chat template content format detection
sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR refines the message conversion that transforms ChatMessage from grpc route_chat_cmpl to the tokenizer.apply_chat_template. To support this, it refactored the current chat_template.rs to accept serde_json::Value instead of redefining a chat_template::ChatMessage. And added a bunch

sglang.title: [router] select first healthy worker on proxied get requests
sglang.author: lun-4
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
when using kubernetes service discovery on the router and a pod restarts, the router keeps a hold of the previous worker. that's fine at a base level, but proxy_get_request does not check if the worker is healthy and instead just selects the first one irrespective of its health state. thi

sglang.title: chore: Initial support for input config files
sglang.author: kushanam
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Reducing and organizing the input parameters for server commands

This PR adds support for YAML configuration files to SGLang server, allowing users to specify server arguments through config files instead of (or in addition to) command-line arguments. This feature follows the same pattern as vLLM's

sglang.title: router-grpc: Add tools processing and other paramters for apply_chat_template
sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR serves as a follow-up of #10832 . It adds full support for Python's tokenizer.apply_chat_template functionality in the Rust router, including support for tools, documents, continue_final_message, and **template_kwargs parameter.
To achieve this in Rust (which lacks Python's flexib

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
…ming output

Purpose
Problem
In non-streaming output mode, DeepSeekV31ToolParser fails to correctly parse multiple tool calls.
For example, given the input:
<｜tool▁call▁begin｜>get_current_time<｜tool▁sep｜>{}<｜tool▁call▁end｜>
<｜tool▁call▁begin｜>big_number_add<｜tool▁sep｜>{"a": 923, "b": 24922}<｜tool▁c

vllm.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Only enable +rms_norm based on aiter if not explicitly disabled
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow up on #23412
If VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER is enabled, and "-rms_norm" is explicitly set in the compilation config, an assertion is going to be thrown that an operation can't be both enabled and disabled.
Changing the enablement logic from the PR to respect explicit disablement.
